digest.component_or_area: close-gate completion verifier and agent worktree guidance
propositions: The close gate runs verify_completion against the committed HEAD of whichever checkout the close command runs from, not origin/main and not necessarily the edited worktree.
Agents get confused because editing in one worktree but running close from the main checkout causes the verifier to read the wrong unchanged tree.
Documentation must factually state that the close-gate verifier reviews the committed HEAD of the invoking checkout and that close must be run from the worktree holding the code.
The cwd/branch decoupling statement in docs/repo-snapshot-gates.md must be reconciled so it no longer contradicts the ref=HEAD close path.
Repo rules and both agent skills must instruct the agent to cd into the created worktree and run all subsequent commands from inside it.
B-layer rule and skill edits must be plain operational instructions without a defense-in-depth rationale, and make lint must pass.
